metrics.narrative: Leandro Damasio quantified the scale of the problem: roughly $600M in addressable spend, of which the current 6-person (growing) procurement team cannot fully manage; roughly 15-20 contracts signed per month; and an admitted inability to state a current contract-compliance rate ('I don't know... I had to really deep dive on the data spend, like, probably one, two days getting Excel files', Avoma, 6 Mar 2026). This establishes real scale but no buyer-set savings or ROI target yet -- that is the business case Leandro still owes his organization.

champion.narrative: Leandro Damasio has behaved like a genuine champion: he initiated and scoped the original discovery call, brought six additional Cinemark stakeholders (Jon Gappa, Duane Flowers, Ekta Matta, John Hunter, Lisa Block, Fabricio Sosigan) to the 31 Mar 2026 demo, asked pointed questions, and confirmed fit. But his engagement has cooled: he replied once by email on 4 May 2026 saying the team was busy and would follow up with commercial inputs 'this week' -- that follow-up never arrived, he went out of office through mid-June per the 10 Jun 2026 rep note, and there has been no further confirmed buyer-side contact since. He is developing, not yet proven durable, and currently the deal's only real thread.

competition.narrative: Cinemark is confirmed to be running a multi-vendor process ('evaluating multiple suppliers then going to RFP', Next Step, 6 Mar 2026; 'trying to understand what's out there in the market', Leandro Damasio, 31 Mar 2026; and a 31 Mar 2026 call note explicitly logs 'Cinemark evaluating Zycus alongside other unnamed procurement solutions currently in demo phase'), but no specific rival has ever been named in any call, task, or Salesforce field (Competitors__c is blank). This is a genuine visibility gap, not a hygiene one -- the shortlist has never surfaced.

sources: Avoma call notes, 31 Mar 2026 (pain_point note, speaker Duane Flowers/Leandro Damasio)
identify_pain.narrative: Pain is rich and buyer-articulated: fragmented spend across Workday (US), Microsoft Dynamics (Brazil) and satellite systems for facilities/MRO, requiring manual Excel pivoting and cross-referencing to do spend analysis ('It doesn't really mean a lot for me on the procurement side... this is why I'm looking for a spend analytics tool', Leandro Damasio, 6 Mar 2026); a manual Smartsheet-based contract repository with home-grown expiration alerts that still risks costly unintentional auto-renewals; and hundreds of redundant vendors in categories like facilities/plumbing with no rationalization tooling. At the 31 Mar demo Leandro confirmed the tailored walkthrough 'definitely covers the very vast majority of our pain points.'

sources: No evidence in Q1-Q3 SOQL fields or Avoma calls
paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics (MSA, SOW, redlining, signatory path) have been discussed on any call or in any field. The deal is still pre-RFP/pre-selection in the enterprise motion, so this is expected at this stage rather than a red flag, but it is genuinely unstarted.

sources: Avoma call notes, 31 Mar 2026 (decision_making, budget note types)
economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer or budget owner is identified in Salesforce (budget-owner fields empty) or on any call. Jon Gappa (SVP Sourcing) is the most senior Cinemark attendee and is named a primary decision maker alongside Duane Flowers and Leandro Damasio (Avoma, 31 Mar 2026), but no evidence shows he or anyone else controls or has been asked to approve the commercial spend. Leandro stated he must still 'go and schedule a demo where I'm gonna bring more people... to see that' and later that the team will 'compare Zycus with other solutions... and reconvene with follow-up questions and budget considerations' -- budget approval is explicitly still ahead of them, not behind them.

decision_process.narrative: Leandro confirmed the team will run a formal RFP ('I will probably go to a formal RFP', Avoma, 31 Mar 2026), and that Procurement, Legal, Finance and Tax will jointly review and approve contracts by category once selected. Immediate next step after the demo was internal alignment: comparing Zycus to other tools, reviewing the deck, and reconvening with questions and budget considerations. No RFP has been confirmed as formally issued as of the most recent Next Step (7 Jul 2026).

decision_criteria.narrative: Leandro explicitly scoped the evaluation to Spend Analytics, CLM, and (potentially) the autonomous tail-spend agent, and explicitly excluded full source-to-pay because Cinemark already runs procure-to-pay through Workday ('I would like to keep focused on the spend analytics, CLM, and potentially on the agent AI capabilities', Avoma, 31 Mar 2026). Specific criteria surfaced: vendor normalization, custom taxonomy separate from finance's, AI-powered clause libraries and redlining, Workday integration, configurable value-based approval workflows (Duane Flowers, 31 Mar 2026), and AI data-governance/private-tenant assurance (buyer's IT stakeholder, 31 Mar 2026). No written, weighted scoring rubric exists yet -- that would come with the formal RFP Leandro says is coming.

blocks.footer: Competitive picture: Cinemark is confirmed to be running a multi-vendor evaluation ahead of a formal RFP, but no specific competitor has ever been named across any call, task, or Salesforce field.

bullets.full: Multi-vendor RFP creates loss risk via competitor preferred-positioning or RFP-scoring-criteria mismatch. Buyer stated 'evaluating multiple suppliers then going to RFP' (Next_Step 2026-03-06) and 'we will probably go to formal RFP' (2026-03-31). No competitor named, so we are blind to competitive positioning. If competitor has incumbent advantage (e.g. existing Workday partnership, legal-CLM install-base at Cinemark, or prior relationship with Jon Gappa/Leandro), they may have inside track on RFP criteria. If RFP scoring weights price or legal-CLM features higher than AI/procurement-analytics (Zycus strengths), we lose despite product fit. Buyer is early exploratory ('just beginning, trying to understand market', 2026-03-31) and has time ('Q3 or Q1 2027'), so no urgency to sole-source Zycus — RFP will run full multi-vendor cycle. Risk: RFP commoditizes evaluation and buyer selects competitor on price, incumbent advantage, or criteria weighting we didn't influence.
bullets.text: Multi-vendor RFP creates loss risk via competitor preferred-positioning or RFP-scoring-criteria mismatch.

bullets.full: Close date 2026-07-31 (24 days out) is not credible. Buyer stated 'ideally Q3 2026 implementation, but could be Q1 2027 depending on many factors; we have time' (2026-03-31) — this is a loose, buyer-conditional timeline, not a locked close. Remaining steps: issue formal RFP (intent stated 2026-03-31, not yet done), complete multi-vendor evaluation, select vendor, build and approve business case with economic buyer, negotiate commercials, execute contract (MSA/legal/InfoSec review). 24 days insufficient for this path. Buyer engagement cooling May-June: last buyer email 2026-05-04 (64 days ago), buyer OOO until late June (2026-06-10 Next_Step), no new contact as of 2026-07-07. Realistic close Q4 2026 or Q1 2027 per buyer's own statement. Risk: deal slips beyond current fiscal planning window or buyer deprioritizes if timeline stretches into 2027.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 2026-02-23, 134 days old. Stage Formal Evaluation with no submission-date progression fields populated. Close date 2026-07-31 is 24 days out. Buyer explicitly stated 'ideally Q3 2026 implementation but could be Q1 2027 depending on many factors; we have time' (Next_Step 2026-03-31 demo), indicating close is conditional and flexible, not locked. Required remaining steps: issue formal RFP (buyer intent stated 2026-03-31, not yet confirmed issued), complete multi-vendor evaluation, select vendor, build and approve business case with economic buyer (unmapped), negotiate commercials, execute contract (MSA, legal, InfoSec). Realistic cycle for this path is 90-180 days. Commercial input sheet requested 2026-05-12, overdue 56 days with no delivery despite buyer promise to follow up 'this week' on 2026-05-04. Last buyer email 2026-05-04, 64 days ago. LastActivityDate 2026-07-01 (6 days ago) shows recent rep touch but no new buyer response logged in Next_Step history as of 2026-07-07. Time-in-stage math: 134 days in Formal Evaluation with no forward progression signals (no shortlist confirmation, no RFP issuance, no EB engagement, no pricing approval). Pace required to close 2026-07-31 is not realistic given RFP cycle and approval chain still ahead. Forward slip likely to Q4 2026 or Q1 2027 per buyer's own timeline.

north_star_verdict.headline: 24 days to close with no credible path: economic buyer unmapped, formal RFP not issued, commercial inputs overdue 56 days, buyer stated Q3 or Q1 2027 timeline, and close date is not defensible against multi-vendor RFP cycle still ahead

flags: Map and engage the economic buyer immediately — Leandro (Sr Director) is champion but cannot approve budget; he must 'present business case to the organization' (2026-03-31). No CFO, CPO, or budget-owner Contact Role, no Who_will_approve_budget__c field populated. Ask Leandro who controls procurement-software budget and request joint call to validate business case and secure sponsorship. Without EB buy-in the deal stalls at approval regardless of product fit.

Identify the competitive field and run battle-card positioning — buyer stated 'evaluating multiple suppliers then going to RFP' (Next_Step 2026-03-06) and 'we will probably go to formal RFP' (2026-03-31) but no competitor named. Ask Leandro who else he's demoing and what he's seen; position Zycus differentiation (AI agents Anna/Merlin/InstaReview, organic procurement-centric platform vs legal CLM or ERP add-ons, private-tenant AI architecture). Make our strengths the RFP anchor by co-authoring his business case around Zycus ROI.
Re-establish live cadence with Leandro Damasio before RFP scoring criteria lock in; nine weeks of silence risks losing influence over how the RFP is scored versus unnamed rivals.

Re-thread beyond Leandro: the other six 31 Mar 2026 attendees (Jon Gappa, Duane Flowers, Ekta Matta, John Hunter, Lisa Block, Fabricio Sosigan) have gone silent; single-contact dependency on Leandro is the biggest execution risk heading into a formal RFP.

Buyer engagement cooling: last buyer email 2026-05-04 (64 days ago); commercial input sheet requested 2026-05-12, no response, 57 days overdue; rep follow-up 2026-06-10 noted buyer OOO until late June; rep invited to DFW event 2026-07-07, awaiting reply

Close date 2026-07-31 (24 days) not credible: buyer stated 'ideally Q3 2026 or Q1 2027 depending on many factors; we have time'; remaining path (RFP, EB approval, MSA/legal, contracting) requires 90-120 days; realistic close Q4 2026 or Q1 2027
Blind competitive position: multi-vendor RFP planned but no competitor identified; risk of incumbent advantage or price-driven loss in RFP
best_practice_check.summary: The single highest-leverage move is reopening the commercial conversation the buyer itself asked for and has left unanswered for roughly eight weeks; the second is breaking out of single-threaded contact with only the champion; the third is delivering the ROI/business-case material Zycus already promised to unblock Leandro's internal approval.

competitive_position.summary: Cinemark is confirmed to be running a multi-vendor evaluation ahead of a formal RFP, but no specific competitor has ever been named across any call, task, or Salesforce field. The rep is actively re-engaging (a Horizon invite went out 7 Jul 2026), but confirmed buyer-side momentum has been essentially flat since a single email reply on 4 May 2026.

deal_risk.text: Close date of 31 Jul 2026 is not credible: Leandro himself said 'ideally Q3 2026... could be Q1 2027 depending on many factors' (31 Mar 2026), and no formal RFP has been confirmed issued yet.
No economic buyer has been identified; without one, Leandro's business case has no confirmed approver and could stall or be rejected at that gate.
A multi-vendor RFP is planned with zero named competitors, risking a price-driven down-select on criteria Zycus has not had a chance to influence.

win_position.text: Seven Cinemark stakeholders spanning SVP to category manager (Jon Gappa, Leandro Damasio, Duane Flowers, Ekta Matta, John Hunter, Lisa Block, Fabricio Sosigan) attended the tailored 90-minute Spend+CLM+AI demo on 31 Mar 2026, and the champion confirmed it covers 'the very vast majority of our pain points' (Avoma, 31 Mar 2026).

No economic buyer or budget owner is identified anywhere in Salesforce or on any call; the business case Leandro must present internally has no named approver (Avoma, 31 Mar 2026; SF budget-owner fields empty).
A commercial input sheet requested on 12 May 2026 remains outstanding as of the 7 Jul 2026 Next Step, roughly 8 weeks overdue, and the last confirmed buyer reply was 4 May 2026 (66 days ago).

A formal RFP is planned with an entirely unnamed multi-vendor field ('I will probably go to a formal RFP', Leandro Damasio, 31 Mar 2026), leaving Zycus with zero visibility into who it is actually competing against.

deal_momentum.text: The only two-way buyer engagement since the 31 Mar 2026 demo was a single email reply on 4 May 2026; there has been no confirmed buyer response in the 66 days since.
Rep-logged Next Step entries since 12 May 2026 (commercial follow-up, an out-of-office note on 10 Jun 2026, and a Horizon invitation on 7 Jul 2026) are one-way outreach with no logged buyer reply.
A fresh re-engagement attempt (Horizon/DFW event invitation to Jon Gappa and Leandro Damasio) went out 7 Jul 2026, 2 days ago, aiming to reopen multi-threaded contact.

customer_commitment.text: Champion Leandro Damasio personally drove the evaluation, brought six additional Cinemark stakeholders to the 31 Mar 2026 demo, and confirmed the solution matches Cinemark's stated priorities.
That commitment has not translated into forward motion since: the requested commercial input sheet (12 May 2026) is still outstanding and Leandro has initiated no contact since his 4 May 2026 reply.

hard.ais_why:
• The March 31 2026 demo confirmed alignment on the AI value proposition: consolidated spend analytics, AI-driven CLM and autonomous negotiation were validated against Cinemark's pain points. The buyer acknowledged the AI fit but IT raised a governance constraint requiring AI models to run in a private tenant with no Cinemark data leaving that environment. That is sophisticated AI engagement with guardrails.
• Buyer questions on AutoClass AI classification, Merlin intake orchestration via Teams, MS Word InstaReview for clause-level risk, and Anna autonomous negotiation for tail-spend were active but reactive to the vendor flow. SVP Jon Gappa was in the room with Senior Directors Damasio and Flowers, with the close framed as comparing Zycus against other solutions rather than committing to AI as the differentiator.
• AI importance is rated Neutral and the description records no AI adoption currently with prospect curious in an ROI-driven posture. Four meetings on record, including a deep-dive 89-minute session, but the messaging is about building a generic procurement business case rather than an AI-led transformation. With no AI champion, this sits cleanly on the Curious side.

deltas.to: Buyer is price-shopping to build business case and will run formal RFP, creating price-comparison risk. Initial SaaS pricing shared 2026-05-20 (Next_Step) but b
deltas.subject: Buyer is price-shopping to build business case and will run formal RFP, creating price-comparison risk. Initial SaaS pricing shared 2026-05-20 (Next_Step) but buyer requested commercial input sheet 2026-05-12 and has not provided inputs or confirmed pricing fit. Buyer stated 'we need sense of price to create business case' (2026-03-31) — price is a gate to approval, not yet cleared. Multi-vendor RFP ('evaluating multiple suppliers') will commoditize evaluation and drive price competition. If competitor underbids Zycus, buyer may favor them even with weaker product fit. deltas.to: Multi-vendor RFP creates loss risk via competitor preferred-positioning or RFP-scoring-criteria mismatch. Buyer stated 'evaluating multiple suppliers then going
deltas.subject: Multi-vendor RFP creates loss risk via competitor preferred-positioning or RFP-scoring-criteria mismatch. Buyer stated 'evaluating multiple suppliers then going to RFP' (Next_Step 2026-03-06) and 'we will probably go to formal RFP' (2026-03-31). No competitor named, so we are blind to competitive positioning. If competitor has incumbent advantage (e.g. existing Workday partnership, legal-CLM install-base at Cinemark, or prior relationship with Jon Gappa/Leandro), they may have inside track on RFP criteria. If RFP scoring weights price or legal-CLM features higher than AI/procurement-analytics (Zycus strengths), we lose despite product fit. Buyer is early exploratory ('just beginning, trying to understand market', 2026-03-31) and has time ('Q3 or Q1 2027'), so no urgency to sole-source Zycus — RFP will run full multi-vendor cycle. Risk: RFP commoditizes evaluation and buyer selects competitor on price, incumbent advantage, or criteria weighting we didn't influence.

deltas.subject: Close date 2026-07-31 (24 days out) is not credible. Buyer stated 'ideally Q3 2026 implementation, but could be Q1 2027 depending on many factors; we have time' (2026-03-31) — this is a loose, buyer-conditional timeline, not a locked close. Remaining steps: issue formal RFP (intent stated 2026-03-31, not yet done), complete multi-vendor evaluation, select vendor, build and approve business case with economic buyer, negotiate commercials, execute contract (MSA/legal/InfoSec review). 24 days insufficient for this path. Buyer engagement cooling May-June: last buyer email 2026-05-04 (64 days ago), buyer OOO until late June (2026-06-10 Next_Step), no new contact as of 2026-07-07. Realistic close Q4 2026 or Q1 2027 per buyer's own statement. Risk: deal slips beyond current fiscal planning window or buyer deprioritizes if timeline stretches into 2027.

deltas.subject: Co-author quantified business case this week to convert pain into budget justification — buyer confirmed product fit ('covers vast majority of pain points' 2026-03-31) but has no ROI numbers. Pain is qualitative (manual Excel 1-2 days per analysis, no vendor normalization, Smartsheet CLM, 300+ plumbing vendors). Buyer accepted Zycus offer to 'support business case with cycle-time reduction, visibility' (2026-03-31) but nothing delivered yet. Build ROI model: vendor rationalization savings (normalize Coca-Cola entities, rationalize 300 plumbing vendors by region), tail-spend negotiation 3-7% savings (Anna agent), contract compliance enforcement (reduce maverick spend), cycle-time reduction (CLM demo case study showed 10% reduction in 4 months). Deliver it this week so Leandro has ammo for internal approval and the business case anchors the RFP to Zycus strengths.
